# Ideas Pertaining to a Pure Phenomenology (Ideas I)

*[Husserl](../philosophers/husserl.md) · 1913 · *Ideen zu einer reinen Phänomenologie**

The statement of phenomenology's transcendental turn: Husserl sets out the phenomenological reduction, the suspension of the natural attitude, and the analysis of consciousness as intentionality, open onto the objects it intends.
